channel.title: Pixel Art on RaihnForge
channel.description: Recent content in Pixel Art on RaihnForge

item.title: UF: VERG – Castleroid: 02
item.description:
The Castleroid exercise is exactly what it sounds like — a deliberate study in the tile-based graphic language of classic Castlevania and Metroidvania games.

The goal isn’t to clone anything. It’s to deeply understand how those games construct a sense of place with limited pixels. Every tile is a decision about value, silhouette, and repetition. Doing this work by hand — without shortcuts — teaches things that reading about pixel art never can.

item.title: UF: VERG-Delvroid
item.description:
Unchosen Forge: Validity Exercise in Retro Graphics - Delvroid

Pixel Zeld-A-Like

Zelda Like Dungeon Adventure Art Study. Concept is to create usable assets in the vein of a classic retro Zelda style romp.

For this project I’d like to create a complete set of Dungeon assets in the like of the classic Zelda 1 game. I’m making the pixel graphics 400% larger so that I test the viability of swapping them with HD graphics.

item.title: UF: VERG-Castleroid: 01
item.description:
Unchosen Forge: Viability Exercise Retro Graphics - Castleroid

Tile Pallet System

Work continues on my Castlevania 1 retro redux.  The Hall is coming along nicely.  Trying to focus on values, colors, and getting the tile system in place.  Much of the work isn’t shown in the simple graphic above, but I’m challenging myself to create a system that would theme of the environment to allow for a great deal more geometry.

item.title: Cheermotes for BaseTrade TV
item.description:
BaseTrade TV is a StarCraft 2 broadcast organization with a strong community presence on Twitch. Cheermotes are animated emotes tied to the Bits cheer system — small, expressive animations that play when viewers donate.

The brief called for something that bridged the pixel art aesthetic with modern emote readability. The result was a hybrid style: the chunky geometry and limited palette of pixel art, but with enough polish to work at Twitch emote sizes.

item.title: WIP — Mecromage Animation Demo
item.description:
Mecromage has been in development for longer than I care to admit. It started as a concept in 2013 and has gone through more iterations than I can count — which is both a testament to stubbornness and a record of how much my craft has grown.

The animation demo collects a set of sprite-based assets built in Adobe Animate CC: walk cycles, idle states, and combat motions. These aren’t the final designs, but they represent a significant milestone in moving from concept to something that actually moves.

item.title: WIP — Mecromage Animation Demo
item.description:
Mecromage has been in development for longer than I care to admit. It started as a concept in 2013 and has gone through more iterations than I can count — which is both a testament to stubbornness and a record of how much my craft has grown.

The animation demo collects a set of sprite-based assets built in Adobe Animate CC: walk cycles, idle states, and combat motions. These aren’t the final designs, but they represent a significant milestone in moving from concept to something that actually moves.
